release_notes: Fixes two GitHub direct-installer bugs surfaced by an end-to-end install/uninstall test on a fresh Windows box. (1) install_chairside_ready_alert.ps1 and uninstall_chairside_ready_alert.ps1 each contained em-dash characters (U+2014) in comments and one Write-Host string. The .ps1 files are saved as BOM-less UTF-8; Windows PowerShell 5.1 reads BOM-less .ps1 files in the system default codepage (Windows-1252), which misdecodes the 3-byte UTF-8 em-dash as 'a-tilde / euro / right curly quote'. The stray curly quote put the tokenizer into an unterminated-string state, and by the time the parser reached the Add-Type @"..."@ here-string deep in install_chairside_ready_alert.ps1 it could no longer recognize the opener -- the install bailed with 'Missing using directive' / 'using statement must appear before any other statements'. All em-dashes replaced with '--' so the files are pure ASCII; encoding is now irrelevant. (2) The uninstaller's kill step searched all processes for the substring 'chairside_ready_alert' in their command lines -- and matched its own PowerShell process (whose -File argument is '...\uninstall_chairside_ready_alert.ps1'), Stop-Process -Force'd itself, and exited before reaching the registry/install-dir cleanup. From Apps & Features the entry looked like it uninstalled successfully (exit 0 from the relocation parent) while in fact nothing was removed. The kill-step regex is now anchored to 'chairside_ready_alert\.py' (requires the .py extension, which only the Python app process has) and explicitly excludes anything matching 'uninstall_chairside_ready_alert' plus the current $PID as belt-and-suspenders. Both fixes apply only to the GitHub direct-installer path; Microsoft Store builds (PyInstaller MSIX) ship the EXE directly and never run these scripts. Both the root-level scripts (referenced by version.json for direct-installer updates) and the bundled copies at 'Chairside Ready Alert Windows Installer/' are updated in lockstep.
